generated_comment:
Ramesh: "Sunita, Priya ab 27 ki ho gayi hai. Aur kitna intezar karegi? Achha kama rahi hai, khush bhi hai, theek hai. Par uski shaadi ka kya?
 Agar woh aise hi 30 ki ho gayi, toh phir achhe rishte milenge kya? Aur hamare yahan log toh abhi se baatein banana shuru kar diye hain, 'beti abhi tak kunwari hai?' karke."
Ramesh: "Humein usse ab seriously baat karni padegi. Ladkiyon ki shaadi ki ek umar hoti hai. Baad mein sirf pareshani hoti hai."
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The character overtly expresses that there is a 'right age for girls to get married' and that delaying will cause 'trouble.' He explicitly cites societal pressure and gossip as a reason to push his daughter towards marriage, implying her value is tied to her marital status and age.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
